name: Drone Flight Time Prediction
description: Calculate precise flight time using battery capacity, voltage, and power consumption with real-world correction factors.
formula: Flight Time (hours) = (C × V × η × D) / P | Flight Time (min) = hours × 60 | Real-World = Ideal × Profile Multiplier
formulaDescription: Calculates flight time using battery capacity (C in Ah), voltage (V), system efficiency (η), usable discharge fraction (D), and average power consumption (P). If power is unknown, calculates from drone weight and power-per-kg ratio. Applies real-world correction factors based on flight profile.

example.explanation: A 5000 mAh (5 Ah) battery at 11.55V with 85% efficiency and 80% usable discharge provides 39.27 Wh. At 500W average consumption, ideal flight time is 4.71 minutes. For normal flight profile (0.6 multiplier), real-world flight time is approximately 2.83 minutes.

faqs.question: What is system efficiency and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: System efficiency (typically 80-90%) accounts for power losses in ESCs, wiring, connectors, and conversion stages. Default is 85%. Higher precision systems achieve 90%+.

faqs.question: What is usable discharge fraction?
faqs.answer: LiPo batteries should not discharge completely (damages battery). Usable discharge is typically 0.8 (80%), meaning you discharge down to 20% remaining. Some conservative users use 0.7 (70%).

faqs.question: How do I measure average power consumption?
faqs.answer: Use a battery monitor or power meter during actual flight. Hover power is typically 40-50% of max power. For mixed flight, use 50-70% of max power.

faqs.question: Why is real-world time different from ideal time?
faqs.answer: Real-world correction factors account for take-off transients, acceleration, wind resistance, and altitude changes. Light hovering is closest to ideal (0.75×), while aggressive racing is far from ideal (0.35×).

faqs.question: What is power required per kg (W/kg)?
faqs.answer: This varies by drone design and flight style. Quadcopters typically need 100-200 W/kg for sustained flight. Racing drones and heavy payloads may need more. Default 150 W/kg is a reasonable estimate.

faqs.question: Should I use calculated power or measured power?
faqs.answer: Always prefer measured power from flight logs or battery monitors. Calculated power (from weight × W/kg) is an estimate useful for predictions before flight. Combined estimation gives best predictions.
